questions.question: Every physical process of weather is accompanied by, or is a result of:
One weather phenomenon which will always occur when flying across a front is:

options.b: A large precipitation area, if the frontal surface is steep
options.c: A large temperature change, especially at high altitudes
options.d: The presence of clouds, either ahead of or behind the front

questions.question: If there is a thunderstorm activity in the vicinity of an airport at which you plan to land, which hazardous and invincible atmospheric phenomenon might be expected on the landing approach:
What conditions are necessary for the formation of thunderstorms?

options.a: Lifting force, high humidity and unstable conditions
options.b: High humidity, high temperature and cumulus cloud
options.c: Low pressure, high humidity and cumulus cloud
options.d: Lifting force, high temperature and unstable conditions

questions.question: Thunderstorms reach their greatest intensity during the:
Which weather phenomenon is always associated with a thunderstorm?
One in-flight condition necessary for structural icing to form is:
The International Standard Atmosphere assumes:

options.a: Temperature of +15 degrees F, pressure 1013.25 mbs.
options.b: Temperature of +15 degrees C, pressure 1012.35 mbs.
options.c: Temperature of +15 degrees C, pressure 1013.25 mbs.
options.d: Temperature of +15 degrees F, pressure 1013.25 in.

questions.question: The Dry Adiabatic Lapse Rate (DALR) assumes a reduction in temperature of:
The Saturated Adiabatic Lapse Rate (SALR) assumes a reduction in temperature of:
If moist stable air is forced up a mountain slope what type of cloud will form?
The transition zone where two different air masses meet is called:
Cold air masses are:

options.a: the same as warm air masses
options.c: heavier than warm air masses therefore wedge under warm air masses
options.d: lighter than warm air masses therefore slide over warm air masses

questions.question: What is the most recognised discontinuity across a front?
What type of precipitation is associated with cumuliform clouds?
What kind of weather is associated with Cumulonimbus clouds?
The International Standard Atmosphere uses as its reference point:

options.a: Mean Sea Level temperature and pressure

questions.question: When air temperature and dew point coincide, it is likely to give rise to:
What type of cloud characterises stable air?
The lowest cloud base is likely with:
The three stages of thunderstorm are in the following order:
A line on a map joining places of equal sea level pressure is called:
Severe turbulence is most likely within:
Carburettor icing is most common within:
What type of aviation hazard is most often associated with lenticular clouds?
Rotor or roll cloud warns of:
The formation of advection fog is most likely with:

options.a: high relative humidity, clear skies, light winds 2-8 knots
options.b: low relative humidity, Overcast, winds 10-15 knots
options.c: warm moist air, moving over cold ground with a temperature below the air's dewpoint
options.d: light winds with cold air moving over warm ground

questions.question: The formation of radiation fog is most likely with:

options.a: high relative humidity, clear skies, light winds 2-8 knots
options.b: warm moist air, moving over cold ground with a temperature below the air's dewpoint
options.c: light winds with cold air moving over warm ground
options.d: low relative humidity, Overcast, winds 15-20 knots

questions.question: In Northern Hemisphere wind circulation around a low pressure system is:
An approaching warm front will usually give:

options.a: light winds becoming calm with intermittent drizzle
options.b: Overcast, raising cloud base, heavy rain
options.d: overcast, reducing cloud base, rain becoming continuous

questions.question: Constant starboard drift indicates an aircraft is flying from:
Friction causes surface wind to:

options.a: weaken in strength and back in direction
options.b: increase in strength and veer in direction
options.c: remain steady with a variable direction
options.d: increase in strength and back in direction

questions.question: In the Northern Hemisphere the passage of either a cold or warm front causes:
Flying from a low to high pressure system will lead to:
An altimeter will over-read if not corrected when flying from:

options.b: a low to high pressure system

questions.question: An altimeter will under-read if not corrected when flying from:
The passage of a cold front will cause temperature to:
Surface wind will at night:

options.a: back in direction and reduce in strength
options.b: veer in direction and reduce in strength
options.c: back in direction and increase in speed strength
options.d: its direction will remain constant with variable strength

questions.question: The code GR on a Met report represents:
The symbol 9999 in a Met report denotes:
